title: cmt — Conventional Commits configuration
description: Configuration file for cmt, the zero-dependency Conventional Commits CLI. Place as .cmt.json at your repository root.

$schema.description: JSON Schema pointer — enables editor intellisense. Leave as-is.

customTypes.description: Additional commit types appended after the built-in ones (feat, fix, docs, …).

type.description: The type keyword used in the commit header, e.g. 'wip' or 'security'. Must be lowercase letters, digits, hyphens or underscores.

emoji.description: Optional emoji displayed in 'cmt log' and the commit picker.

semver.description: The Semantic Versioning impact this type carries. Use 'none' for types that don't affect the public API.

description.description: Short human-readable description shown in the interactive commit picker.
examples: Work in progress — not ready for review

scopes.description: Suggested scopes shown in the 'cmt commit' scope picker. Users can also type a custom scope or skip. Leave empty for free-text input only. E.g. ['auth', 'api', 'ui', 'db'].

items.description: A single scope name. Lowercase, no spaces.

rules.description: Lint rules applied by 'cmt lint' and the commit-msg git hook.

maxHeaderLength.description: Maximum character length of the commit header line. Headers longer than this emit a warning. Default: 72.

requireScope.description: When true, 'cmt lint' will error if no scope is present in the commit header. Default: false.

allowBreakingChanges.description: Commit types allowed to carry a BREAKING CHANGE footer or '!' marker. Default: ["feat", "fix"].

disallowUpperCaseDescription.description: When true, descriptions starting with an uppercase letter are an error (not just a warning). Default: false.

disallowTrailingPeriod.description: When true, descriptions ending with a period are an error (not just a warning). Default: false.

customTypes.description: Work in progress — not ready for review
